_meta.updated: 2026-05-20 (Phase 11 ship — voice-directed drafting: multi-variant opening paragraphs. cv-backend: opening_variants.py prompt (4 named patterns, single JSON-array call), GenerateOpeningVariantsRequest/Response schemas, /internal/generate-opening-variants endpoint (synchronous, HTTP 200), SYSTEM_BODY_ONLY + USER_TEMPLATE_BODY_ONLY prompts, _generate_body_with_chosen_opener() in generator.py, chosen_opening?: string added to GenerateCoverLetterRequest. Migration 027: DROP+ADD constraint adds 'picking' to status CHECK; opening_variants/chosen_opening/discarded_openings JSONB columns added. Web: cvBackend.ts (OpeningVariant, GenerateOpeningVariants* interfaces + function); /cover-letter POST phase-split: generateOpeningVariants() called before DB insert — cv-backend failure returns 502 without leaving stuck rows; row created with status='picking' + opening_variants payload; returns {letter_id, status:'picking', variants}; /cover-letter/[letter_id]/pick POST: auth → fetch letter (PickLetter cast) → 409 if not picking → find chosen → compute discarded → re-resolve CV/voice/key/story/JD → PATCH {chosen_opening, discarded_openings, status:'pending'} → generateCoverLetter(chosen_opening:) → 502 rolls back to 'picking'. Audit fix d61c048: company_name reads jobs.company not cover_letters.company_hook_text. CoverLetterPanel: extended CoverLetterRow + status union, Realtime SELECT updated, isPicking derived state, handlePick(), stacked-card VariantPicker UI with pattern_label + 'Use this opener' buttons. Commits: 8dbcf8c (11b), d61c048 (audit fix).) — Worker curl_cffi session (2026-05-20): scripts/fetch_jd.py + src/lib/curlfetch.ts + seekDirect curl_cffi upgrade (replaced got-scraping; location normalisation) + careerjet two-phase integration (API+tracking-URL-immediate-resolution+curl_cffi-JD-enrichment). SEEK: 22 jobs/run at $0 from Fly datacenter, no proxy. Careerjet: 20 jobs + 10/11 JDs at $0. Apify actor: fallback only, $0. Dockerfile: python3 + curl_cffi added. OPS-13+OPS-14 captured. Worker service status updated from copied_from_jobtrackr → active.
_meta.purpose: Single source of truth for the JobTrackr-CV integration project. Read every session start. Update immediately when state changes. DESIGN.md is the architectural plan; this is the live state.

project.name: JobTrackr-CV
project.summary: JobTrackr v2 — absorbs cv-magic's CV-tailoring pipeline as an internal feature module. ONE domain (Vercel auto URL), ONE user identity (Supabase), ONE database. The Python pipeline service is invisible plumbing on Fly.io.
project.framing: cv-magic is no longer a separate product. It is a block of functionality inside JobTrackr. Users only ever see jobtrackr-cv-*.vercel.app. The Fly.io Python service has no public domain.
project.navigation: Same-tab (no new tabs). Analysis pages are JobTrackr routes: /jobs/[id]/analyze, /jobs/[id]/analyze/[run_id].
project.domain: Vercel auto URL (deferred custom domain). Production JobTrackr at its own URL is untouched.

CVVersion.notes: Partial unique index on (user_id) WHERE is_active = true. PDF stored at cvs/{user_id}/{cv_id}.pdf in Supabase Storage. cv_text extracted by cv-backend via pypdf.

AnalysisRun.notes: Mirrors cv-magic AnalysisRun with company_id renamed to job_id. step_status = 7-key JSON tracking each pipeline step. cv-backend writes via service-role; frontend reads via RLS + Realtime.

UserIntegration_extension.change: Allow provider values 'anthropic' and 'openai' in addition to existing 'apify'
UserIntegration_extension.notes: Reuses AES-256-GCM crypto helper. Validation endpoints: api.anthropic.com/v1/models, api.openai.com/v1/models.

UserPreference.notes: Added in Phase 7.5 via Migration 020. Stores contact details and portfolio projects for stamping on tailored CVs.

relations: belongs_to: User (many rows per user — no UNIQUE on user_id)
Story.notes: Added in Phase 10.2.a via Migration 022. One row per story, multiple per user. Atomic overwrite on re-extraction: web route deletes all user stories then inserts new batch (single INSERT — atomic in Postgres; no partial write possible). extraction_timestamp is datetime with no DB default — set by cv-backend so all rows in a batch share the same value, enabling Phase 10.2.b batch queries: WHERE user_id = ? AND extraction_timestamp = MAX(...). numbers is JSONB [{metric, value}], tags is text[] (advisory vocabulary, not enum-enforced). Composite index on (user_id, extraction_timestamp DESC). RLS: auth.uid() = user_id. cv-backend writes via service-role.

relations: no user_id — global shared cache, not per-user
CompanyResearch.notes: Added in Phase 10.3 via Migration 024. company_id is a text slug computed by make_company_slug() (cv-backend) and replicated in JS (web route). RLS: auth.role() = 'authenticated' for SELECT only — all authenticated users can read; service-role key used for writes (bypasses RLS). Tavily system-level key (TAVILY_API_KEY env var) used for web search; gracefully degrades to httpx scraping when absent. Fact selection is deterministic (keyword scoring, no AI) via /internal/select-company-fact. Cache TTL checked in web route, not cv-backend.

fields: story_id (FK → stories ON DELETE SET NULL)
opening_variants (jsonb — array of OpeningVariant: {id, text, pattern_label})
chosen_opening (text — verbatim text of picked P1 variant)
discarded_openings (jsonb — array of unchosen OpeningVariant rows, for telemetry)
relations: belongs_to: Story (nullable, ON DELETE SET NULL)
CoverLetter.notes: Added in Phase 10.4 via Migration 025. Extended in Phase 11 via Migration 027 (new columns: opening_variants, chosen_opening, discarded_openings; 'picking' added to status CHECK). One row per generated letter per (user_id, job_id) pair. Lifecycle: status='picking' → user selects opener → status='pending' → cv-backend generates P2-4 → status='completed'/'failed'. cv-backend writes via service-role; frontend reads via RLS + Realtime. generation_status JSONB tracks sub-steps. RLS: auth.uid() = user_id. Realtime: added to supabase_realtime publication.

relations: belongs_to: User (unique — one profile per user)
VoiceProfile.notes: Added in Phase 10.1 via Migration 021. UNIQUE on user_id — new sample silently overwrites existing. voice_sample_raw is NEVER returned in any GET response path or logged. voice_sample_source is text (not enum) with default 'in_app_capture' — extensible to file upload / audio in later phases. fingerprint is JSONB storing the 14-key VoiceFingerprint structure. RLS: auth.uid() = user_id with check.

web.stack: Next.js 14, Tailwind, TanStack Query, Supabase browser client (Realtime only)

worker.role: Job discovery pipeline only — NOT used for CV analysis.
worker.stack: Node.js 22, TypeScript, BullMQ, Python curl_cffi subprocess
worker.notes: Primary SEEK path: seekDirect adapter via curl_cffi (Chrome 124 TLS, no proxy needed from Fly). Apify Seek actor: automatic fallback only, never ran in production. Careerjet: two-phase — Phase 1 API listings + immediate tracking-URL resolution (Node fetch redirect:manual) → stable careerjet.com.au/clk URLs stored in DB; Phase 2 curl_cffi JD enrichment. Confirmed production 2026-05-20: SEEK 22 jobs/run + Careerjet 20 jobs + 10/11 full JDs at $0.

cv_backend.stack: FastAPI, Python 3.11, async-only, ReportLab for PDF, Supabase service-role client

internal_analyze.response: 202 Accepted { run_id }; pipeline runs in BackgroundTasks

internal_select_company_fact.response: { ranked_facts: [{fact_text, score, source_field}] } — deterministic, no AI call

build_state.next_action: Phase 11 shipped (commits 8dbcf8c, d61c048). Phase 12 TBD — candidates: stale-variants detection (variants_generated_at column; 'Refresh options' if research fresher), per-paragraph regeneration UI, cover letter outcome tracking (accepted/rejected/no_response), or delivery-format polish. Read PHASE-11-STALE-VARIANTS planning_note and BUG-3 for the two most natural starting points.
completed: Phase 0 — preview env verified
Phase 1 — migrations 010/011/012 applied; tables live + Realtime publication active
Phase 2 — cv-backend deployed; /health 200, signed /internal/analyze returns 202
Phase 3 — CV upload UI + flow working for PDF (DOCX has known issue BUG-1, parked)
Phase 4 — BYOK keys (Anthropic/OpenAI/DeepSeek) UI + routes
Phase 5 — Analyze button wired end-to-end; JD analysis step runs live (polling fallback added because Realtime delivery is unreliable)
Phase 5.5 — Manual JD edit + contact email
Phase 6 — Wire remaining steps + result cards
Phase 7 — Tailored CV PDF + viewer (Download PDF button via pre-rendered ReportLab)
Phase 7.5 — Profile settings (contact details + portfolio projects)
Phase 8 — Analyses tab + history view grouped by job
Phase 8.5 — Integrations page consolidated (AI keys merged in); separate AI-keys nav removed

Phase 8.6 — cv-magic detail layout parity: CvJdMatchingCard table + ATS named sub-scores + 4-bucket FeasibilityCard with Evidence/Suggested rewrite + Tailored ATS-lift circle layout + Print/.md/PDF download trio on TailoredCvCard
Phase 9.1 — 5-theme system: Default / Classic / Gilded Noir / Notion / Clay. ThemeProvider + FOUC guard + CSS variable token architecture. Theme picker in sidebar.
Phase 9.2 — Sidebar redesigned to match cv-magic reference: brand-colour left-bar active indicator, lucide icons, per-theme font/radius/width tokens.
Phase 9.3 — All Tools pages (CV library, Analyses, Profile/Settings, Integrations, Analysis run) ported to cv-magic design language: glass cards, label-luxury, page-title/subtitle typography.
Phase 9.4 — Job table: theme-aware hover, row actions collapsed to Analyze + tri-dot (⋮) dropdown (Edit JD, View analysis, Mark applied, Dismiss). Edited JD label green.
Phase 9.5 — Job filter bar refactored: JobStatusTabs on own row + unified JobFilterBar (secondary filters + sort) on single nowrap row. Inline styles override .field{width:100%} bug.

Phase 9.6 — ProviderPicker accordion replaces AiKeyCard on Integrations page. Each card: radio (preferred), key input/connect/replace/disconnect, model pills (PATCH to DB). Preferred provider → localStorage; AnalyzeJobButton reads it + sends as body param; API honours override or falls back to priority.
Phase 9.7 — Theme-aware scrollbar: thumb uses color-mix(brand 35%) so each theme renders its own colour.

Phase 9.8 — Global hex color sweep: replaced all hardcoded #D0D7DE/border, #F6F8FA/surface-2, #0969DA/brand, #1F2328/text etc. with CSS var() tokens across 21 files so all elements respond to theme switches.
Phase 9.9 — ⋮ RowMenu dropdown fixed: createPortal + position:fixed so menu escapes overflow:hidden table wrapper and animation containers. All options now visible.
Phase 9.10 — Soft Skills layout fixed: SkillRow changed from flex-wrap (label+chips on same line) to flex-col; label now always on own line consistent with Technical/Domain Knowledge.
Phase 9.11 — Apply/Dismiss reliability: switched from startTransition (silent error swallow) to direct await with try/catch + UI revert on server action failure.
Phase 9.12 — CV re-categorise: added '↺ Categorise skills now' button to CvSkillsBlock + new POST /api/cv/[id]/recategorise route; auto-retries with provider default model if stored model fails.

Phase 9.13 — Sign out fixed: middleware was intercepting POST /auth/signout and redirecting to /dashboard because /auth/* rule had no exclusion for /auth/signout. Added exclusion. Also added visible 'Sign out' text label next to icon.
Phase 9.14 — Analysis history shows final (tailored) score; bare percentage with initial in grey parens when both exist.
Phase 9.15 — Active CV sorts to top of list immediately when Set Active clicked.
Phase 9.16 — OpenAI non-chat model fallback: cv-backend client.py detects 'not a chat model' error and retries with gpt-4o. Web routes also retry with ai_model=null on failure.

Phase 9.17 — Brand-tinted theme borders: Clay --border #F5BECE (pink), Notion --border #C1B7EC (purple), Gilded Noir --border #5C4D23 (gold-dark). Table lines, card outlines, form boxes now match each theme's brand.
Phase 9.18 — ProviderPicker OpenAI models updated to real chat models: GPT-5.1 (recommended), GPT-4o, GPT-4o mini, GPT-4 Turbo, o3-mini. Removed fictional gpt-5.2/gpt-5.2-pro/gpt-5-mini.

Session A cleanup (2026-05-16) — Pre-cover-letter hygiene: (1) Migration renames: 005_vertical_filter→006_vertical_filter, 009_possible_duplicate→009_add_possible_duplicate_dedup_status. No prod impact — migrations are CLI-untracked, applied manually via SQL editor. (2) Dead components deleted: AiKeyCard.tsx (superseded by ProviderPicker), JobCard.tsx, JobSortBar.tsx (both superseded by JobTable/JobFilterBar in Phase 9), ToggleActiveButton.tsx (never wired up). (3) scrapeJobUrl.ts kept but marked as DEFERRED-1 in deferred_features; deferred comment block added to file header. (4) bullmq + ioredis confirmed legitimate — server-side only, used by job-discovery pipeline queue trigger in run/route.ts and actions.ts; do not remove. (5) Test artefacts removed from git: 2× Jora CSV, discovered_slugs.json, jora_scraper.py, verify_slugs.py, 3× jora_test JSON, test-update.js, fix-dead-links.js. Patterns added to .gitignore with explanatory comment.

Session B orchestration layer (2026-05-16) — Permanent subagent + slash command infrastructure. (1) .claude/agents/ created: planner (haiku-4-5, Read/Grep/Glob, plan-only), auditor (opus-4-7, Read/Grep/Glob/Bash read-only, PASS/FAIL structured review), migration-checker (sonnet-4-6, Read/Grep/Glob/Bash, pre-migration safety checker). (2) .claude/commands/ created: /plan (invokes planner, stops for approval), /audit (invokes auditor, halts on FAIL), /ship (graph.json → commit → push → summary ritual), /handoff (generates copy-pasteable next-session block). (3) CLAUDE.md updated: Model Routing section (routing table + Opus-is-reviewer rule), Session Management Rules section (60% threshold + /handoff protocol). (4) Smoke test passed: planner and auditor both verified to run in isolated agent contexts with correct models, producing structured output.

Session C prompts.py modular split (2026-05-17) — Split cv-backend/app/services/ai/prompts.py (1413 lines, 12 constants) into a prompts/ package: 6 step modules (jd_analysis.py, cv_jd_matching.py, keyword_feasibility.py, ai_recommendations.py, tailored_cv.py, cv_skill_categorisation.py) + __init__.py with backward-compatible re-exports. All 6 consumer import paths unchanged. All 5 PLAN-1 auditor corrections applied (exact line boundaries, skill_categoriser consumer included, atomic commit, local-only verification, graph.json in same commit). Three smoke tests passed. Session required graceful mid-session recovery when tailored_cv.py (~970 lines) exceeded executor context capacity — OPS-4 heuristic captured.

Session D documentation pass (2026-05-17) — Created docs/architecture.md (tech stack, data flow, HMAC boundary, BYOK, endpoints, deployment, constraints), docs/database.md (exhaustive schema reference: 10 tables, 2 storage buckets, functions, triggers, extensions — derived from all 20 migration files), and docs/cover-letter-spec.md (full 10-part cover letter generation spec, verbatim). Auditor returned PASS_WITH_NOTES; three fixes applied: HMAC encoding precision note in architecture.md, illustrative model-name disclaimer in cover-letter-spec.md Part 4, ai_provider/ai_model added to AnalysisRun.fields in graph.json. OPS-5 operational note captured. Primary criterion confirmed: a future planner can plan Phase 1 (Voice Fingerprint Module) from the three docs + graph.json alone.

Phase 10.2.a — Story Extraction and Storage (2026-05-18) — Migration 022 (stories table: JSONB numbers, text[] tags, datetime extraction_timestamp with no DB default, composite index on (user_id, extraction_timestamp DESC), set_updated_at trigger, RLS users_own_stories). cv-backend: app/schemas/stories.py (StoryNumber, Story with datetime extraction_timestamp, ExtractStoriesResponse), app/services/stories/story_extractor.py (privacy boundary block, _MAX_CV_CHARS=12_000, full Pydantic validation per story, extraction_timestamp injected by extractor not model), app/services/ai/prompts/cover_letter/story_extraction.py (STORY_EXTRACTION_SYSTEM with 8 BAD/GOOD calibration pairs + empty-result instruction, STORY_EXTRACTION_USER_TEMPLATE), story_extraction_fixtures.py (RICH_CV/SPARSE_CV/GENERIC_CV with P1-P4 criteria + async run_fixture_check()), POST /internal/extract-stories endpoint (HMAC-protected, mirrors extract-voice-fingerprint pattern), prompts/__init__.py re-exports updated. Web: cvBackend.ts (Story/StoryNumber/ExtractStoriesPayload/ExtractStoriesResult interfaces + extractStories() wrapper, 90s timeout), POST /api/user/stories/extract route (session auth → active CV from cv_versions → BYOK key resolve → cv-backend call → atomic delete+single-statement INSERT). Auditor verdict: PASS_WITH_NOTES — 3 doc fixes applied post-audit (extraction_timestamp docstring corrected to datetime, SPARSE_CV P5 verb-ban criterion removed from prose and never in list, prose P-count now matches list at 4). Smoke test passed 2026-05-18 (commit 2bdb835): 8 stories extracted and persisted for test user — correct UUIDs, timestamps, domains, and years verified in Supabase Table Editor. Phase 10.2.a fully complete.

Phase 10.3 — Company Research Pipeline (2026-05-18) — Migration 024 (company_research: text PK slug, jsonb facts/voice_signals/hiring_intel, research_quality_score float, search_skipped bool, auth.role()='authenticated' RLS — no user_id). cv-backend: app/schemas/company.py (CompanyFacts/VoiceSignals/HiringIntel/CompanyResearch Pydantic models + request/response schemas), app/services/company/slug.py (make_company_slug — deterministic slug, 80-char cap), app/services/company/quality_scorer.py (compute_quality_score — 3-signal formula, no AI), app/services/company/researcher.py (Tavily search + httpx scrape + AI distillation + stale-flag injection on RecentEvent), app/services/company/fact_selector.py (keyword scoring, story_matcher pattern, no AI), app/services/ai/prompts/cover_letter/company_research.py (bad/good fact framing prompt). TAVILY_API_KEY added to config.py + .env.example. POST /internal/research-company + POST /internal/select-company-fact added to internal.py. Web: cvBackend.ts (ResearchCompany* + SelectCompanyFact* typed wrappers, 120s/10s timeouts), POST /api/company-research (trigger+cache), GET+POST /api/company-research/[company_id] (read+refresh), POST /api/company-research/facts/select (fact ranking). Auditor PASS_WITH_NOTES: two post-audit fixes applied (dead TTL_SECONDS constant removed, Exception narrowed to AIClientError). Smoke tests C/D/E all passed: completed+skipped=true, cached on 2nd call, ranked facts returned deterministically. Phase 10.3 fully complete. Session I post-merge fixes (commit 8882fc4): /api/company-research auth+DB lookup parallelised with Promise.all (cache-hit latency ~600ms faster); /api/company-research/facts/select refactored to accept {job_id} and resolve company_id/jd_text/cv_text server-side (ownership: jobs→search_profiles→user_id, JD: manual_jd_text→analysis_run fallback→422, CV: cv_versions is_active=true).

Phase 10.2.b — Story Matching and UI (2026-05-18) — Migration 023 (replace_stories RPC: plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER function wrapping DELETE+INSERT in one transaction, eliminating the Phase 10.2.a DELETE-INSERT gap; service_role-only execute grant). cv-backend: app/services/stories/story_matcher.py (deterministic keyword scoring: frozenset tokenisation, ~150 English function-word stop-words, overlap normalised by JD size, +0.15 numbers bonus, capped at 1.0, sorted descending), app/schemas/stories.py extended (id: Optional[str]=None added to Story, MatchStoriesRequest/ScoredStory/MatchStoriesResponse added), POST /internal/match-stories endpoint (no AI call; logs jd_len+story count only). Web: cvBackend.ts (MatchStoriesStory/MatchStoriesPayload/ScoredStory/MatchStoriesResult interfaces + matchStories() 10s wrapper), GET /api/user/stories (two-query batch: max timestamp → batch), POST /api/user/stories/match (job ownership chain → JD resolution: manual_jd_text → analysis_run fallback → 422 → story batch → cv-backend → Map score merge → sorted response), PATCH /api/user/stories/[id] (ownership check + tag/one_line constraints enforced), extract/route.ts updated to call rpc('replace_stories',...) replacing two-call pattern. UI: StoriesClient.tsx (335 lines: StoryCard sub-component, multi-expand Set<string>, inline tag editing state machine, 90s re-extract in-flight label, state-update-in-place on save/re-extract), voice/page.tsx extended (server-side two-query story fetch, StoriesClient rendered below VoiceCaptureClient with hr divider). Auditor: initial FAIL (MatchStoriesStory missing title+year — Pydantic 422 on every match call); both fixes applied; re-verified manually; TypeScript clean. Phase 10.2.b fully complete.

Session E — Phase 10.1 (Voice Fingerprint Module, 2026-05-18) — Migration 021 (voice_profiles: RLS, unique user_id, updated_at trigger) applied manually. cv-backend: app/schemas/voice.py (VoiceFingerprint Pydantic model, 14 fields with type constraints), app/services/voice/trust_scorer.py (pure function, TrustScore NamedTuple, 23 AI-tell phrases, deterministic), app/services/voice/trust_scorer_fixtures.py (6 smoke-test fixtures), app/services/voice/voice_fingerprint.py (extract_voice_fingerprint, full Pydantic validation, server-side-only error logging, no_training=True), app/services/ai/prompts/cover_letter/ (new subdirectory: voice_fingerprint.py prompt), app/services/ai/client.py (no_training: bool = False threaded through complete_json → complete → _anthropic_complete/_openai_complete; OpenAI store=False implemented; Anthropic TODO pending SDK support), app/routes/internal.py (POST /internal/extract-voice-fingerprint). Web: web/src/lib/cvBackend.ts (extractVoiceFingerprint wrapper), web/src/app/api/user/voice-profile/route.ts (GET omits voice_sample_raw; POST: key lookup/decrypt/call/upsert; TODO Phase 1.5 trust regression warning), web/src/components/cv/VoiceCaptureClient.tsx (paste disabled, word counter, TrustBadge), web/src/app/(dashboard)/dashboard/voice/page.tsx (Server Component), web/src/components/SidebarNav.tsx (Mic icon + My Voice link). Auditor verdict: PASS_WITH_NOTES — 20/21 checkpoints clean; one fixture calibration bug fixed post-audit (OBVIOUS_AI expected_length_score 1.0→0.7, ~165 words corrected to ~136 words). All 6 smoke-test fixtures PASS after fix. OPS-6 captured. cv-backend redeployed 2026-05-18 (commit c931c07 → image deployment-01KRW3TWARNA0PWS6PKZ4FF2PS, version 17, region syd). /internal/extract-voice-fingerprint confirmed live via OpenAPI spec check. Browser E2E smoke test (sign in → /dashboard/voice → submit sample → verify fingerprint) pending manual verification by user.

Phase 10.4 — Cover Letter Generation (2026-05-18 + 2026-05-19) — Session 1: migration 025 (cover_letters table: status enum, generation_status JSONB, pass_1/2/3 text+model cols, gate scores, quality_flags JSONB, is_stale, RLS, Realtime publication), cv-backend three-pass pipeline (generator.py + model_router.py + quality_gates.py + 3 pass prompts + gate_1_honesty), /internal/generate-cover-letter endpoint. Session 2: web POST/GET routes, CoverLetterPanel UI with Realtime subscription, wired into analysis run page. Session J (refactor, 2026-05-19): replaced 3-pass pipeline with single-call architecture after end-to-end quality testing showed mechanical-feeling output. New design: one call to user's chosen model with voice sample + CV + story + company fact + four-paragraph intent rubric + anti-patterns; honesty gate rewritten for scope (candidate-claims only) and leniency (paraphrase tolerance). Company research is now a prerequisite (web POST returns 422 + action=research_company if missing; UI shows 'Research [Company]' button calling existing /api/company-research). Low-quality research falls back to JD-hook with warning. P1 spec bans role-first openers and demands substantive candidate-perspective claim (with 3 different-domain example shapes to prevent verbatim copy + formula entrenchment). P3 spec uses contrastive WEAK/STRONGER examples for structural variety + 4 named texture categories (colleague reaction / what changed afterwards / what was hard / specific moment). Deleted: model_router.py, quality_gates.py, pass_1/2/3_*.py prompts. DB schema unchanged; old rows keep 6-key generation_status, new rows use 2-key. quality_flags merge in generator preserves web-layer pre-writes. Commits 8044aa2, 07e3b2b, 0c3679f, fd42d47, f12732a.

Phase 10.5 — Delivery Template + PDF Export (2026-05-19, Session K) — Initial ship (Haiku, commits 0c21b19→caefadf): Migration 026 (hiring_manager column on jobs), suburb/postcode added to user_preferences contact_details, JobEditModal hiring_manager field, /api/jobs/[id]/cover-letter/[letter_id]/{template,download} routes, CoverLetterPanel rewritten with jsPDF client-side rendering (A4, 0.8in margins, 11pt Helvetica). Post-ship Opus audit caught 5 real bugs that all shipped: (1) /template route was dead code, never wired; (2) ownership-fail returned 401 instead of 403; (3) hardcoded 'Australia' line in employer block; (4) 'M' user-specific fallback initial in filename slug; (5) job.location existed and was unfetched, so employer block was missing city/state. Three behavioural bugs also fixed: editedBody not reset on regenerate (silent stale-edit send), hiring-manager name duplicated in employer block + salutation, name positioned BELOW company instead of above (inverted AU convention). Plus two product-feedback fixes: dropped RE: line entirely (redundant in one-job letters), and company research now auto-triggers transparently with didAutoResearch loop guard (no manual 'Research [Company]' button). Cleanup commits: f45a3df (5 bug fixes + shared coverLetterTemplate.ts helper extracted, 122-line net reduction), 8f270e6 (hiring-manager dedup + reorder), 463aa0c (wire job.location), ec5f086 (drop RE: + auto-research). All pushed and Vercel-deployed. Final ship state: AU-standard format, single-page fit verified, accessible modal (role=dialog, Escape, backdrop click, autoFocus), shared template helper at web/src/lib/coverLetterTemplate.ts.

Worker curl_cffi infrastructure + SEEK direct + Careerjet integration (2026-05-20) — curl_cffi (Chrome 124 TLS impersonation) bypasses Cloudflare from Fly.io datacenter IP without any proxy — confirmed in production. New files: scripts/fetch_jd.py (Python subprocess: Chrome 124 JA3/ALPN via curl_cffi, --proxy + --no-redirect flags, single JSON-line stdout), src/lib/curlfetch.ts (TypeScript wrapper: curlFetch() full-body fetch + curlRedirect() header-only probe). seekDirect.ts: replaced got-scraping with curlFetch; added normaliseSeekLocation() stripping ', Australia' suffix from state-level locations; 22 SEEK jobs/run at $0 from Fly datacenter without any proxy. careerjet.ts: Phase 1 — Careerjet API listings + immediate tracking-URL resolution (Node native fetch(redirect:'manual') at CONCURRENCY=5 resolves jobviewtrack.com/v2/<hash> → careerjet.com.au/clk/<hash> within seconds of API call, before tokens expire); Phase 2 — curl_cffi JD enrichment (10/11 full descriptions merged, 421–6846 chars each). Careerjet.com.au URLs stored in DB — links are permanent and clickable in UI. Apify actor path preserved as automatic orchestrator fallback — never ran. Dockerfile: python3-minimal + pip3 install curl_cffi + COPY scripts/fetch_jd.py. Production run confirmed: SEEK 22 jobs + Careerjet 20 jobs, Apify cost $0.

Phase 11 — Voice-directed drafting: multi-variant opening paragraphs (2026-05-20) — cv-backend: opening_variants.py (VARIANTS_SYSTEM + VARIANTS_USER_TEMPLATE with 4 named patterns: Story Hook, Conviction Statement, Mirror-Back, Before/After Contrast; Pattern D key constraint: contrast must be in thinking/method not metric results); schemas/cover_letter.py (OpeningVariant, GenerateOpeningVariantsRequest/Response, chosen_opening optional on GenerateCoverLetterRequest); services/cover_letter/variants.py (generate_opening_variants: single JSON-array call, temperature 1.0 for gpt-5* else 0.9, 700 max tokens, validates ≥3 variants); generator.py (_generate_body_with_chosen_opener using SYSTEM_BODY_ONLY + USER_TEMPLATE_BODY_ONLY; coherence constraint: P2 must build on P1's frame; run_cover_letter_pipeline branches on chosen_opening present/absent); /internal/generate-opening-variants endpoint (synchronous HTTP 200, HMAC-protected). Migration 027: adds 'picking' to status CHECK (DROP+ADD constraint pattern — text not enum); adds opening_variants/chosen_opening/discarded_openings columns. Web: cvBackend.ts (OpeningVariant/GenerateOpeningVariants* typed wrappers, 60s timeout); /cover-letter POST phase-split (variants generated before DB insert — failure returns 502 without leaving stuck rows; row created with status='picking'; returns {letter_id, status:'picking', variants}); /cover-letter/[letter_id]/pick POST (new route: auth → PickLetter cast workaround for Supabase type lag → 409 guard → find chosen/discarded → re-resolve prerequisites → PATCH to 'pending' → generateCoverLetter(chosen_opening:) → 502 rolls back to 'picking'; audit fix d61c048: company_name from jobs.company not cover_letters.company_hook_text); GET route updated to include new columns in SELECT. CoverLetterPanel: OpeningVariant interface, extended CoverLetterRow ('picking' in status union + 3 nullable fields), polling pauses during 'picking', handlePick(), stacked-card VariantPicker UI (pattern_label in small caps, 'Use this opener' button, disabled/loading during in-flight). Opus audit: PASS_WITH_NOTES — one blocking fix applied (d61c048), three non-blocking notes deferred. Commits: 8dbcf8c (11b web), d61c048 (audit fix).

decisions.decision: Two services, not a port. cv-backend stays Python.
decisions.rationale: Preserves 100% of pipeline logic; ReportLab PDF unportable without diverging output.

decisions.decision: Shared Supabase project with production JobTrackr.
decisions.rationale: New tables are isolated from production code paths. FK from analysis_runs.job_id → jobs.id requires same DB.

decisions.decision: BYOK Anthropic + OpenAI; no platform AI credits.
decisions.rationale: Zero ongoing cost; user controls spend.

decisions.decision: HMAC-signed internal API between JobTrackr and cv-backend.
decisions.rationale: Simpler than JWKS; both services are private; one caller.

decisions.decision: Realtime via Supabase postgres_changes on analysis_runs row.
decisions.rationale: Same pattern as cv-magic; no polling tax.

decisions.decision: Stale strategy: new run on (user, job) marks prior runs is_stale=true. Re-run is user-triggered only.
Production JobTrackr deploy stays on its own repo; this project deploys to its own Vercel+Fly apps.
decisions.rationale: Zero blast radius until explicit promotion decision.

decisions.decision: AI keys sidebar link removed; key management consolidated into Integrations page.
decisions.rationale: Reduces sidebar clutter; one fewer nav item for what is a rare settings action.

decisions.decision: Preferred AI provider stored in localStorage (not DB); passed as optional body param to /api/jobs/[id]/analyze.
decisions.rationale: No migration needed; preference is per-browser and lightweight. API falls back to priority order (Anthropic > OpenAI > DeepSeek) if param absent or provider not connected.

decisions.decision: AiKeyCard component removed from Integrations page; absorbed into ProviderPicker accordion.
decisions.rationale: Single component owns the full provider management lifecycle: connect, replace, disconnect, model selection, preferred flag.

decisions.decision: Dropdowns/menus inside overflow:hidden containers use createPortal + position:fixed.
decisions.rationale: Table rows use overflow:hidden for the collapse animation; absolute-positioned menus get clipped. Portal to document.body with fixed coordinates from getBoundingClientRect() is the only escape.

decisions.decision: AI model fallback: if stored model fails categorisation, retry once with ai_model=null (cv-backend provider default). Web routes handle this; cv-backend also catches 'not a chat model' and falls back to gpt-4o.
decisions.rationale: Users may have stored invalid/legacy model names from earlier ProviderPicker versions. Silent retry means they still get categorisation without manual intervention.

decisions.decision: OpenAI model list restricted to real chat-completions-compatible models: GPT-5.1, GPT-4o, GPT-4o mini, GPT-4 Turbo, o3-mini.
decisions.rationale: Previous list had fictional gpt-5.2/gpt-5.2-pro/gpt-5-mini names that caused 404 API errors. Completions-only models (gpt-3.5-turbo-instruct etc.) excluded — they fail at the /v1/chat/completions endpoint.

decisions.decision: Cover letter generation uses a single call to the user's chosen model (BYOK), not a hard-coded cheap/expensive tier split. Voice sample (verbatim) drives register; intent-based 4-paragraph rubric drives structure; anti-patterns list steers away from generic openers; one post-generation honesty gate (same model) with one retry on flag.
decisions.rationale: Original 3-pass pipeline (skeleton→voice transfer→burstiness, with 3 deterministic gates) produced mechanical-feeling output: literal 'Worth it.' fragments copied verbatim from prompt examples, uniform 'I [verbed] X, which Y' sentence structures the burstiness gate did not catch, weak 'Hi, I admire' openers that survived multiple banned-phrase patches. Root cause: the pipeline was performing humanness rather than achieving it — pass-3 instructions to 'add a fragment' produce literal copies; pass-2 statistical voice fingerprint cannot replicate voice; pass-1 'hook → bridge' formula locks in cliché two-sentence openers. Replacing the pipeline with one rich call + verbatim voice sample + intent rubric + anti-patterns produced materially better output verified on Ampol and One51 letters. Trade-off: user's model choice determines quality ceiling (was previously masked by hard-coded expensive tier). Honest about what we can deliver; user controls the cost/quality dial via their integration settings.

planner.role: Pre-task planning. Produces structured 10-section plan. Never executes.

auditor.role: Phase-boundary code review. Returns PASS / PASS_WITH_NOTES / FAIL. Never modifies code.

migration_checker.role: Pre-migration safety check. Verifies CLI state, filename collisions, risk profile.

slash_commands.plan: .claude/commands/plan.md — invokes planner subagent, stops for user approval
slash_commands.audit: .claude/commands/audit.md — invokes auditor subagent, halts on FAIL
slash_commands.ship: .claude/commands/ship.md — phase wrap-up: graph.json → commit → push → summary

orchestration_layer.model_routing_policy: Documented in CLAUDE.md ## Model Routing. Main=sonnet-4-6, Planning=haiku-4-5, Auditing=opus-4-7 (reviewer only, not executor), Migration=sonnet-4-6.

deferred_features.title: Tier 4 user-initiated JD URL fetch
deferred_features.status: built, not wired up — no API route or UI calls this
deferred_features.description: User pastes a job URL into the manual JD edit flow; utility fetches and pre-populates the JD text field via JSON-LD → OpenGraph → HTML parse chain.
deferred_features.distinct_from: cv-backend/services/scraping/jd_scraper.py — that runs during analysis from a known source; this is a pre-analysis, user-triggered fetch.
deferred_features.to_activate: Create POST /api/jobs/scrape-url calling scrapeJobUrl(), returning { jdText, source }. Wire to 'Fetch from URL' button on JobEditModal.
deferred_features.reconsider: When manual JD edit flow gets enhanced (e.g. Phase 10+ or cover letter feature needs richer JD input).

deferred_features.title: Multi-source job scraping architecture for scale (1000s of users)
deferred_features.status: designed, not built — current MVP does naive per-search fetches; OK for test circle of 10-20 users, breaks at scale
deferred_features.description: Current state: a fresh user search triggers live API calls to each source. At 1000 users this hits all rate limits within hours: Careerjet free tier 500/day, Adzuna 1000/month, SEEK Apify ~$225/day at 1000 users × 3 keywords × 3 sources. Designed solution: shared global jobs pool refreshed by scheduled crawler on popular keyword clusters (Tier 1 every 4h, Tier 2 daily, Tier 3 on-demand), user searches filter the pool instantly instead of triggering live fetches. Dedup priority: SEEK > Careerjet > Adzuna (match on normalize(title)+normalize(company)+city); always store all source URLs on the merged row so user sees 'Apply on SEEK · Also on Careerjet'. Visa keyword scan runs once at description-fetch time, stored on row, enables instant 'show sponsorship-friendly jobs only' filter — the JobTrackr differentiator.
deferred_features.distinct_from: Current per-user live fetch in cv-backend — fine for 10-20 test users, will break at production scale.
deferred_features.to_activate: After test circle (10-20 users) validates the AIN + Data Analyst flow: (1) add columns to jobs table — source, source_id, dedup_key, all_sources JSONB, visa_keywords, may_sponsor, requires_pr; index on dedup_key; (2) build Careerjet client module (no Apify needed — uses inline curl_cffi); (3) build description-time visa scanner (single regex pass); (4) build scheduled crawler on Fly cron (every 4h for Tier 1 keywords × major cities); (5) refactor search endpoint to read pool first, on-demand fetch only on cache miss; (6) evaluate decommissioning SEEK Apify actor in favour of inline curl_cffi (curl_cffi was proven 2026-05-20 to extract full descriptions from seek.com.au/job/{id} pages with no Apify dependency — would save ~$225/day at 1000 users if stable at volume).
deferred_features.reconsider: When test circle (10-20 users) is validated AND user count starts approaching the free-tier rate limits — likely around 50-100 active daily users. Earlier triggers: any source returns 429 rate-limit error; Apify monthly bill exceeds budget.

description_extraction.careerjet: curl_cffi works → 5,418-14,366 chars (no Apify, no proxy)
description_extraction.jooble: Cloudflare blocks /desc/ pages — needs Apify residential proxy

source_comparison_snapshot_2026-05-20.jooble_verdict: Skip — 88-93% of AU results come from a single source (swooped.co). Not real aggregation.

operational_notes.title: Migration tracking is CLI-untracked
operational_notes.detail: All migrations applied manually via Supabase SQL editor — supabase link has never been run, no config.toml exists. If supabase link is ever set up, run supabase migration repair to mark all 20 migrations as applied before using any CLI workflows (db push, branching, etc.). Renaming local migration files (as done 2026-05-16) has zero production impact while CLI-untracked.

operational_notes.title: bullmq + ioredis in web/package.json are legitimate
operational_notes.detail: Imported by web/src/app/api/profiles/[id]/run/route.ts and web/src/lib/actions.ts for the job-discovery pipeline BullMQ queue trigger. Both are server-side only (Next.js API route + server action). They do not enter the browser bundle. Do not remove them.

operational_notes.title: Slash commands in .claude/commands/ are user-facing — not agent-triggerable
operational_notes.detail: Files in .claude/commands/ define slash commands typed by the human (e.g. /plan, /audit). Agents cannot invoke slash commands via the Skill tool — that returns an error. Agents invoke subagents directly via the Agent tool, passing the subagent's role prompt explicitly. The slash command files document the protocol and standardise human-initiated invocations; they do not execute themselves.

operational_notes.title: Executor session context limit — large single-file writes
operational_notes.detail: Sonnet executor sessions handle single-file writes up to ~600 lines reliably. Files larger than ~800 lines risk context pressure. Symptoms: re-reading source files without progress, claiming 'continuing now' without producing output, ignoring 'are you stuck' prompts. Mitigation: when a plan involves writing a single file >800 lines, the planner should either propose splitting that file further OR flag it as a dedicated single-task session with no other work in the same context. Heuristic: when an agent re-reads the same file twice in a row without producing output, it IS stuck regardless of what it claims. Discovered: Session C, 2026-05-17, attempting to write tailored_cv.py (~970 lines) after already writing 4 other modules in the same session.

operational_notes.title: Audit acceptance criterion for documentation work
operational_notes.detail: The framing 'could a future planner act from these docs alone, without reading source?' has proven effective at surfacing ambiguities in documentation. It caught three real issues in Session D (HMAC encoding precision, fictional model names in spec, missing analysis_runs fields in graph.json) that a standard correctness review would have missed. Use this framing as the primary acceptance criterion whenever documentation is the deliverable.

operational_notes.title: Fixture-based smoke tests must verify the fixtures themselves, not just the function under test
operational_notes.detail: In Session E, the OBVIOUS_AI fixture declared expected_length_score: 1.0 and a comment claiming '~165 words', but the text was actually 136 words — placing it in the 100–500 intermediate band (score 0.7, not 1.0). The trust scorer was correct; the fixture was wrong. The auditor's smoke test run caught this. Lesson: when writing fixtures that include expected computed values (word counts, score buckets), always compute the value programmatically before writing the fixture rather than hand-counting. Hand-counts drift as text is edited. For word-count-sensitive thresholds specifically: run len(text.split()) in a REPL before committing the expected_length_score.

operational_notes.title: Burstiness floor (0.40) may be calibrated slightly high — defer evaluation to 20+ real samples
operational_notes.detail: Real production sample (848 chars, ~145 words) produced burstiness ratio (stddev/mean sentence length) of 0.36, just below the 0.40 human-writing floor specified in docs/cover-letter-spec.md Part 5. Two interpretations: (a) factual self-introduction is inherently uniform; cover-letter writing will naturally be more varied; (b) the 0.40 floor may be calibrated slightly high for short samples. Do not tune the floor yet. When voice_profiles contains 20+ real samples, evaluate whether 0.36 is an outlier or the norm, then adjust the threshold if needed.

operational_notes.title: 150-word minimum may be safely lowered to 120 — defer to multi-sample validation
operational_notes.detail: Real production sample was 848 chars / ~145 words — right at the lower edge of the 150-word target — and fingerprint quality is strong across all 5 acceptance criteria. Suggests the floor could be lowered to 120 words without degrading output quality. Do not change yet. Collect more samples first and assess whether shorter samples consistently produce comparable fingerprint specificity.

operational_notes.title: MatchStoriesStory TS interface must mirror Story Pydantic schema — all required fields
operational_notes.detail: The TypeScript MatchStoriesStory interface (cvBackend.ts) maps directly to the Pydantic Story schema used in MatchStoriesRequest. FastAPI validates every field at deserialization — a missing required field produces HTTP 422 before the endpoint handler runs. Phase 10.2.b initial audit caught this: title and year were absent, causing every /internal/match-stories call to fail. When Story gains or loses required fields in a future phase, MatchStoriesStory must be updated in sync. A future improvement (noted for Phase 10.3): define a narrower MatchableStory Pydantic schema with only the fields score_stories actually uses, breaking the tight coupling.

operational_notes.title: Scrape pipeline observability layer (Session L)
operational_notes.detail: run_logs gained three fields and one append-only stream over Session L: (1) current_stage text — written by setStage() at each pipeline transition, cleared to NULL on finishRunLog; powers the blue 'Pipeline running' banner. (2) sources_saved jsonb — captured from saveJobs() return; answers 'where did the N saved jobs come from?' without a join. (3) log_lines jsonb + append_run_log_line(rid, line) Postgres function — logContext.ts patches console.log/warn/error once at worker startup; orchestrator calls runLogContext.enterWith({ runLogId }) right after startRunLog so every console call inside the pipeline (orchestrator + adapters + AI helpers) mirrors to log_lines fire-and-forget. UI: LiveRunStatus banner (jobs + runs page), LiveLogConsole collapsible terminal-style scrollback (jobs + runs page), RunNotifier toast mounted in dashboard layout for cross-page run-complete notifications. Polling cadences: LiveRunStatus 3s when active / 12s idle, LiveLogConsole 4s discover + 1.5s tail, RunNotifier 3s. Three migrations land in order: 028 (current_stage), 029 (sources_saved), 030 (log_lines + RPC). Worker must redeploy AFTER 030 is applied or appendLog spams 'function does not exist' warnings.

operational_notes.title: Fast-iteration shipping with a smaller model needs a second-pass review before phase close
operational_notes.detail: Phase 10.5 was implemented and shipped on Haiku with a tight feedback loop (6 commits, four of them cosmetic margin/colour tweaks). The ship was declared after type-check + manual PDF preview. When the user later requested an Opus audit of the same commits, FIVE real bugs surfaced that the shipping session had missed: dead /template route never wired (158 lines), wrong HTTP status (401 vs 403), hardcoded 'Australia' line, user-specific 'M' filename fallback, and an existing job.location column that was never SELECTed so the employer block silently lacked the city/state. Plus three behavioural bugs (stale editedBody on regenerate, hiring-manager duplicated in block + salutation, name inverted under company). Lesson: a smaller-model executor optimising for the tight inner loop reliably misses cross-cutting issues (dead code, HTTP semantics, schema fields the route doesn't fetch, accessibility, edge-case state resets). For phase closes that touch >3 files OR add a new public surface (API route, UI panel, schema column), do a final Opus pass BEFORE writing the ship commit, not as a follow-up. The audit prompt I used worked well: 'review these commits for rooms of improvement, ordered by impact.' Five real fixes for one prompt is worth ~10 minutes of Opus time.

operational_notes.title: curl_cffi bypasses Cloudflare from Fly datacenter IP without any proxy
operational_notes.detail: Python curl_cffi impersonating Chrome 124 (JA3/ALPN TLS fingerprint) bypasses Cloudflare bot protection from Fly.io's datacenter IP (75.102.33.77) without routing through any residential proxy. Confirmed 2026-05-20 in production: SEEK search pages (22 jobs/page) and SEEK job detail pages return HTTP 200. Careerjet.com.au job pages also return HTTP 200 from Fly without proxy. The Apify residential proxy (APIFY_PROXY_PASSWORD env var) remains optional for extra resilience but is NOT required for either source. Architecture: TypeScript spawns python3 scripts/fetch_jd.py as subprocess; Python outputs single JSON line {status, body, url, location} to stdout; TypeScript parses it. Subprocess overhead ~200ms — negligible vs HTTP round-trip. Path resolution: path.resolve(__dirname, '../../scripts/fetch_jd.py') works from both src/lib/ (tsx dev) and dist/lib/ (container build).

operational_notes.title: Careerjet jobviewtrack.com tracking URLs expire in ~1-2 minutes — resolve immediately in Phase 1
operational_notes.detail: Careerjet's API returns one-time-use tracking URLs (jobviewtrack.com/v2/<hash>) that expire within ~1-2 minutes of generation. Fetching them later in Stage 7c (JD enrichment) produces 100% 404s regardless of HTTP client. Fix: resolve tracking URLs IMMEDIATELY after each page fetch using Node's native fetch() with redirect:'manual' to capture the Location header (stable careerjet.com.au/clk/<hash>) without downloading a body. Dispatch 5 resolves in parallel (CONCURRENCY=5) — adds only ~1-2s to each page fetch. Store the stable careerjet.com.au/clk URL in the database — never the jobviewtrack.com URL. JD enrichment in Stage 7c then fetches stable careerjet.com.au pages via curl_cffi. UI links are permanent and clickable. Key implementation: resolveTrackingUrl() uses AbortSignal.timeout(8000) + redirect:'manual'; resolveTrackingUrls() dispatches to concurrent promise pool. Guard in enrichWithCareerjetJDs: filter targets to hostname === 'careerjet.com.au' before fetching — rejects any residual tracking URLs that slipped through.

operational_notes.title: Phase 10.2.a — extraction_timestamp is datetime, SPARSE_CV P5 dropped, INSERT atomicity confirmed
operational_notes.detail: Three decisions captured from audit: (1) extraction_timestamp field in Story Pydantic schema is typed as datetime (not str). FastAPI serialises to ISO 8601 in HTTP responses; web layer receives it as a string and inserts directly as timestamptz. DB migration 022 has no DEFAULT on this column — a missing value produces a NOT NULL violation rather than a silent NULL row. (2) SPARSE_CV fixture P5 criterion (banning 'Helped'/'Assisted' verb starters in one_line) was dropped — banning specific verbs forces overstatement, which is worse than the problem it prevents. P1–P4 remain. (3) Atomic overwrite INSERT atomicity confirmed: admin.from('stories').insert(rows) sends the full batch as a single SQL INSERT ... VALUES (...) statement; Postgres accepts or rejects as one unit. Partial writes cannot occur. The DELETE-INSERT gap (user transiently has 0 stories if INSERT fails after DELETE) is documented with a TODO Phase 10.2.b comment to wrap both calls in a Supabase RPC transaction.

planning_notes.reasoning: Production voice_profiles fingerprint evaluated against Phase 2 acceptance criteria. Tells (5): all specific, actionable, grounded in the sample — directly usable as voice-transfer instructions in Phase 4. Formality (0.55): correctly calibrated, not collapsing to midpoint default. Vocabulary (moderate): matches actual sample register. Rhetorical devices (3): real structural patterns, usable for burstiness injection. Quantitative signals (avg sentence 18 words, stddev 6.4, paragraph openers, intensifiers): all usable. Phase 10.2 story library may proceed.

planning_notes.title: Phase 11 — Voice-directed drafting: multi-variant opening paragraphs
planning_notes.problem: Single-call generator gives the user one shot. If paragraph 1 misses tone or angle, the only recourse is Regenerate, which rewrites the whole letter and burns a model call. The voice fingerprint and company fact are both deterministic inputs — we can cheaply produce 3-4 structurally different openings on the first call and let the user pick.
planning_notes.scope: ONLY the opening paragraph (P1). Paragraphs 2-4 are generated from the chosen P1 in a second call so they cohere with the selected opener. Out of scope: full-letter variants (too expensive, too much UI), per-paragraph regeneration UI (defer to a later phase).
open_design_questions: Should the 3-4 variants come from one model call (JSON array response) or four parallel calls (more entropy, 4x cost)? Recommend single call + JSON array — voice fingerprint pushes diversity better than independent calls of the same prompt.
How do we present variants? Stacked cards with a 'Use this opener' button each? Or a tabbed view? Stacked is more honest about the comparison.
Do we save the rejected variants for telemetry / future fine-tuning? Probably yes — new JSONB column `discarded_openings` on cover_letters.
What happens on Regenerate after the user has picked? Re-pick from same 4, or generate fresh? Recommend fresh — picking should feel intentional, not constrained.
planning_notes.starting_prompt_for_next_session: Read .claude/graph.json then PHASE-11-HANDOFF planning_note. Plan Phase 11 (voice-directed drafting multi-variant opening paragraphs) using the /plan slash command — produce the 10-section plan, then stop for my review.

planning_notes.title: Stale variants edge case — deferred
planning_notes.detail: If a user closes the tab during status='picking' and returns later, the cover_letters row still has status='picking' and opening_variants is populated. CoverLetterPanel reconstructs the picker from the initial prop — same 4 variants are shown without re-generating. Decision: show old variants as-is (option a). The scenario where company research was refreshed between the original variant generation and the user's return (potentially producing better company facts) is acknowledged but rare enough not to build detection logic in Phase 11. Future improvement: add a variants_generated_at timestamp column and compare against company_research.last_researched_at — if research is fresher than variants, offer a 'Refresh options' button. Defer until Phase 12 or later.

open_questions.question: Provider support v1 — both Anthropic and OpenAI, or just Anthropic?
Tailored PDF viewer — inline iframe or download-only?
JD-scrape fallback UX — fail-and-retry or modal-paste?

known_issues.title: GPT-5* family forced to temperature=1 → noisier output than cv-magic intended
known_issues.blocks: Output quality on GPT-5+ models. Does not affect Anthropic, DeepSeek, or GPT-4o.
known_issues.symptoms: JD analysis on GPT-5.x produces less precise / more varied output. cv-magic was designed for 0.1 temperature on analytical steps (JD analysis, CV-JD matching) but OpenAI enforces temperature=1 server-side on the entire gpt-5* family with HTTP 400 'unsupported_value' if anything else is sent.
known_issues.current_workaround: We strip temperature for gpt-5* so the call succeeds; user trades determinism for capability. Lower-friction path: pick Anthropic Sonnet 4.6 which honours per-step temperature exactly.
known_issues.next_steps: Investigate whether OpenAI's `reasoning_effort` param can substitute for temperature control on gpt-5*. Consider per-provider prompt variants tuned for high-temperature output.

known_issues.title: DOCX upload fails with ERR_TIMED_OUT to Supabase Storage
known_issues.symptoms: Browser POST/PUT to ltcqqlfsomqxuwfcxxbe.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/cvs/... aborts at 186ms-2s with net::ERR_TIMED_OUT. Other Supabase requests to the same host (auth, REST) work normally.
tried: Raw fetch PUT to signed URL — fails (assumed, user stopped testing)
known_issues.next_steps: Try a tiny <50KB DOCX to rule out size; inspect Supabase project's storage CORS config; try same upload from a different network/country; consider supabase-js TUS resumable upload as last resort.
known_issues.workaround: Users can convert DOCX to PDF before uploading. PDF path is fully functional.

known_issues.title: company_research distillation produces weak hooks for some employers
known_issues.blocks: Paragraph 2 quality in cover letters when research returns naming history or generic mission statements rather than strategic distinguishing facts.
known_issues.symptoms: Ampol research returned 'Ampol Limited (previously Caltex Australia Limited) returned to its iconic Australian name following shareholder approval on 14 May 2020...' as distinguishing_facts[0]. This is a real historical fact but not a strategic anchor for paragraph 2 — the generator stretched to bridge it ('brand transition → candidate's own career transitions'), producing a workable but slightly forced paragraph. One51 research returned 'Power BI Health Check service' (a specific service offering) and the resulting paragraph 2 was strong and natural. Quality of P2 scales directly with quality of company_research.distinguishing_facts[0].
known_issues.current_workaround: None at generator level — the generator does its job with whatever fact it receives. Users can manually re-run research, but the distillation prompt will likely return similar output.
known_issues.next_steps: Tune the company_research distillation prompt to prefer strategic facts (product expansions, recent strategic moves, distinctive operational characteristics) over historical/naming events. Consider ranking facts by recency and strategic relevance before storing.

known_issues.title: Cover letter delivery layer (Phase 10.5) must fit on a single page
known_issues.blocks: Phase 10.5 (delivery template + PDF export) scoping only — not the current generation pipeline.
known_issues.symptoms: User pasted a 434-word generated letter into MS Word; the body alone filled the full page. Once the AU delivery template wraps it (header block ~5-7 lines, employer block ~4 lines, salutation, RE line, sign-off ~3-4 lines), the combined artefact will overflow a single A4 page.
known_issues.current_workaround: Current word target in generate.py is 250-400 words. When Phase 10.5 is scoped, tighten target to ~280-340 words to leave room for template wrapper. Alternatively (and additively): set type sizing and margins in the PDF render so the wrapped artefact fits comfortably.
known_issues.next_steps: When Phase 10.5 (delivery layer) is scoped: (1) measure actual rendered height of header + body + sign-off in the chosen PDF format; (2) decide whether to tighten word_count_target in generate.py or adjust rendering; (3) update generate.py word target if option 1.

known_issues.title: Jora adapter hangs Chromium on 512MB Fly worker — disabled until memory bump
known_issues.blocks: Jora as a source. Adzuna + Greenhouse + Lever + SEEK (via Apify) still run, so coverage is reduced but not zero. Pipeline runs end-to-end.
known_issues.symptoms: Pipeline reached '[pipeline] stage 2 — fetching: jora' and produced no further [jora] log lines for 3+ minutes. Earlier diagnostic confirmed the await chain stalls inside chromium.launch / context.newPage — when SIGINT closed the browser, the dangling await rejected with 'Target page, context or browser has been closed'. Repro: predictable on jobtrackr-worker shared-cpu-1x:512MB even at worker concurrency=1. Node baseline (~100-150MB) + Playwright Chromium spawn (~200-400MB at load) pushes the VM into swap thrashing and the browser process never makes progress.
known_issues.current_workaround: joraAdapter commented out of worker/src/sources/index.ts. Re-enable when memory is bumped or fetch path is rewritten.
known_issues.next_steps: Pick one: (A) `fly scale memory 1024 -a jobtrackr-worker` (~$3-5/mo extra), uncomment joraAdapter in sources/index.ts, redeploy. (B) Rewrite jora.ts to fetch via undici/HTTP and parse the SSR HTML — Jora used to be fully SSR, may still be partially scrapable without a real browser. (C) Drop Jora entirely if Adzuna+Greenhouse+SEEK coverage is acceptable long-term.
